page: Amazon Web Services r8id.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core) running at 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It is equipped with 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in memory-intensive and computational tasks. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2190.0 and a multi-core score of 19680.0. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 3840.86 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 192.31 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations are supported by a Passmark database score of 23044.96 and Redis SET performance of 85.77 million operations per second. For AI workloads, the hardware-accelerated Intel AMX instructions support LLM inference, processing 333.34 tokens per second for text generation on a small 135M model and 27.85 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8id.12xlarge offers high resource density by bundling high-speed DDR5 memory with fast local NVMe SSD storage on a dedicated hardware platform. This eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput operations, though the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its efficiency for massive parallel AI training. The instance is suited for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput Redis caching layers, local data processing, and medium-scale LLM inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating under the Nitro hypervisor, the server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong performance, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2190.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 85.77 million operations per second. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, enabling efficient local LLM inference, such as 27.85 tokens per second on 7B models. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput caching, and data processing workloads requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR5 RAM, and fast local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
2850 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong database performance with 85.77 million Redis SET operations per second
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated local LLM inference
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
